---
title: "Parks Washroom Predictor"
description: "Predicting the precense of washroom infrastructure in city parks"
date: 2024-09-01
categories: [Python, machine-learning, MDS] 
image: "../../assets/projects/my-project-cover.png"
---

## Overview

Brief context: what was the goal, what data was involved, what did you produce?

Keep this paragraph to 3–4 sentences. The listing card will pull `description:` from the YAML above, so this section can go a little deeper.

## Data

Where the data came from, rough size/shape, any interesting cleaning steps. If it was a school project with a provided dataset, a one-liner is fine.

```python
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("data/raw.csv")
print(df.shape)   # (12540, 24)
df.head()
```

## Approach

What method or model did you use and why? This is where you show your thinking — not just *what* you did but *why* you chose it over alternatives.

## Key findings

A few bullet points or a short paragraph. Visuals go well here — Quarto renders matplotlib, ggplot, and Plotly outputs natively.

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
# ... your actual plot code
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Reflections

What would you do differently? What did you learn that surprised you? This is the paragraph that makes a portfolio project feel real rather than polished-but-hollow.

---

**Code:** [View on GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/repo)  
**Tools:** Python · scikit-learn · pandas · matplotlib
